examples: Most cells other than blood cells live within a fibrous extracellular matrix.
Researchers long thought that it was just another component of the extracellular slime that surrounds all cells.
A molecule (usually a protein) that spans a cell membrane and "receives" extracellular signals and transmits them into the cell.
At the bottom right, orally-active compounds against more conventional extracellular targets is a tricky one.
At the opposite corner are the injectable macrocycles that have to compete in the extracellular space.
